description_long: This awning structure features a long, pitched roof covered in overlapping, fish-scale patterned shingles in shades of gray and tan. The front facade is adorned with four sets of windows, each with ornate wrought-iron railings and small balconies, as well as green-and-white striped fabric canopies below. The side walls are plain, with a weathered, stucco-like finish and vertical brick columns providing structural support. The rear side is unadorned, showing only the same stucco and brick pattern without windows or awnings. The overall design suggests a fixed, architectural installation, likely intended for use above a row of storefronts or as a decorative shelter in an urban environment. The materials and construction indicate durability and a classic European-inspired aesthetic.
description: A long, pitched-roof awning with fish-scale shingles, ornate window balconies, and striped canopies, supported by brick columns and weathered stucco walls.
description_view_0: Side view showing the triangular gable end, plain stucco wall, and brick columns with no windows or awnings visible.
description_view_1: Front view displaying four window sets with wrought-iron balconies and green-and-white striped canopies beneath a shingled roof.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with a plain gable end and no decorative features.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a long, unadorned stucco wall with vertical brick columns and no windows or canopies.
attribute_mounting_location: attached to a building facade above a row of windows or storefronts
attribute_edge_trim_style: straight edge with no decorative trim
attribute_support_structure_type: fixed frame with brick columns and wooden beams
